title: West-Eastern Divan.
content:
Who the song would understand,
    Needs must seek the song's own land.
    Who the minstrel understand,
    Needs must seek the minstrel's land.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, titled "West-Eastern Divan.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
